info.name: MAS 9 - Deploy All Automation Scripts (29)
info.description:
Unified deployment collection for ALL 29 automation scripts from the demo library.

Launch Point Types: ACTION (4), ATTRIBUTE (9), CRONTASK (3), INTEGRATION (1), OBJECT (12)

Scripts:
  - ASSET.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE
  - LOCATIONS.OBJ.INIT
  - PM.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE
  - PO.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE
  - PO.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.AFTER
  - PO.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.BEFORE
  - PR.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE
  - PR.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.AFTER
  - PR.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.BEFORE
  - WORKORDER.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE
  - WORKORDER.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.AFTER
  - WORKORDER.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.BEFORE
  - ASSET.FLD.SERIALNUM.VALIDATE
  - LOCATIONS.FLD.STREETADDRESS.VALIDATE
  - PM.FLD.FREQUENCY.VALIDATE
  - PO.FLD.STATUS.ACTION
  - PO.FLD.TOTALCOST.VALIDATE
  - PR.FLD.REQUIREDDATE.VALIDATE
  - PR.FLD.STATUS.ACTION
  - WORKORDER.FLD.STATUS.ACTION
  - WORKORDER.FLD.STATUS.VALIDATE
  - CRON.INV.REORDER
  - CRON.PM.GENERATE.WO
  - CRON.WO.AUTOCLOSE
  - ASSET.ACTION.DOWNTIME
  - PR.ACTION.AUTOAPPROVE
  - WORKORDER.ACTION.ESCALATION
  - WORKORDER.ACTION.NOTIFY
  - PUBLISH.MXPO.EXTEXIT.OUT

Folders:
  1. Deploy - Creates script + launch point(s) in one API call
  2. Verify - Confirms each script exists with its launch point
  3. Update - Push source code changes to existing scripts
  4. Rollback - Delete scripts and their launch points
  5. Cron Setup - Create cron task definitions (for cron scripts only)

NOTE: Cron task scripts require TWO steps:
  Step 1: Deploy the script (folder 1)
  Step 2: Create the cron task definition (folder 5)
  Step 3: Create cron task instance + set schedule via Maximo UI

Integration scripts bind by name convention - no launch point needed.

Environment: MAS9-Demo

request.description:
UNIFIED SCRIPT DEPLOYMENT COLLECTION
=====================================

29 scripts | 5 launch point types | Single API per script

HOW TO USE:
1. DEPLOY: Run requests in '1. Deploy All Scripts' folder.
   Organized by type: Object, Field, Cron, Action, Integration.
   Each POST creates script + launch point in ONE call.

2. VERIFY: Run '2. Verify All' to confirm deployments.

3. UPDATE: Use '3. Update Source Code' to push changes.
   Set :id path variable from the verify response.

4. ROLLBACK: Use '4. Rollback (Delete)' to remove scripts.
   Set :id path variable from the verify response.

5. CRON SETUP: For 3 cron task scripts ONLY.
   After deploying the script, create the cron task definition,
   then add a cron task instance in the Maximo UI.

LAUNCH POINT TYPES:
- OBJECT (12): Before/after save on MBO objects
- ATTRIBUTE (9): Field-level validate/run-action
- ACTION (4): Workflow/escalation triggers
- CRON (3): Scheduled execution (needs cron task def)
- INTEGRATION (1): Name-bound, no launch point needed

body.raw:
{
  "autoscript": "ASSET.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
  "description": "Validate install date, calculate depreciation, sync parent location, audit trail",
  "scriptlanguage": "jython",
  "active": true,
  "source": "# ==============================================================================
# Script Name:      asset.obj.save.before
# Description:      Object save script for the ASSET object. Performs multi-rule
#                   validation and auto-population on save:
#                     1. Validates INSTALLDATE is populated when STATUS changes
#                        to OPERATING.
#                     2. Auto-calculates REPLACECOST using straight-line
#                        depreciation based on PURCHASEPRICE and asset age.
#                     3. Synchronizes LOCATION with parent asset's location
#                        when PARENT changes.
#                     4. Requires SERIALNUM when ASSETTYPE is "FLEET".
#                     5. Logs all field changes to an audit trail stored in
#                        the ASSETMETER longdescription.
#
# Object:           ASSET
# Launch Point:     Object Launch Point
# Event:            Save (Before Save)
# Active:           Yes
#
# Variables:        None (uses implicit mbo variable)
#
# Launch Point Configuration:
#   Name:           ASSET_OBJ_SAVE
#   Type:           Object Launch Point
#   Object:         ASSET
#   Event:          Save
#   Execution Point: Before Save
#
# Usage Notes:
#   - REPLACECOST depreciation uses a configurable useful life (default 20 years).
#     Modify USEFUL_LIFE_YEARS to adjust for your organization.
#   - The audit trail is appended to ASSETMETER records with METERNAME = "AUDIT".
#     Ensure this meter exists in your domain configuration.
#   - Parent location sync only fires when PARENT actually changes, not on every save.
#   - This script is CLIENT-AGNOSTIC and contains no customer-specific prefixes.
#
# Dependencies:
#   - ASSETMETER object with METERNAME domain containing "AUDIT"
#   - Standard ASSET attributes: STATUS, INSTALLDATE, PURCHASEPRICE, REPLACECOST,
#     PARENT, LOCATION, ASSETTYPE, SERIALNUM
#
# Author:           Demo Library
# Version:          1.0
# Last Modified:    2026-03-01
# ==============================================================================

from psdi.mbo import MboConstants
from psdi.server import MXServer
from psdi.util.logging import MXLoggerFactory
from java.util import Calendar, Date
from java.text import SimpleDateFormat

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logger setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
logger = MXLoggerFactory.getLogger("autoscript.asset.save")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
USEFUL_LIFE_YEARS = 20       # Default useful life for straight-line depreciation
MIN_REPLACE_COST  = 0.0      # Floor value for replacement cost
AUDIT_METER_NAME  = "AUDIT"  # Meter name used for the audit trail

# ==============================================================================
# Helper Functions
# ==============================================================================

def get_field_change_text(mbo_rec, field_name):
    """Return a formatted change string if the field was modified, else None."""
    if mbo_rec.getMboValueData(field_name).isModified():
        old_val = mbo_rec.getMboInitialValue(field_name)
        new_val = mbo_rec.getString(field_name)
        return "%s: '%s' -> '%s'" % (field_name, str(old_val), str(new_val))
    return None


def get_years_since(install_date):
    """Calculate the number of full years between install_date and today."""
    if install_date is None:
        return 0
    now = Calendar.getInstance()
    install_cal = Calendar.getInstance()
    install_cal.setTime(install_date)
    years = now.get(Calendar.YEAR) - install_cal.get(Calendar.YEAR)
    # Adjust if we haven't reached the anniversary yet this year
    if (now.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR) < install_cal.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR)):
        years -= 1
    return max(years, 0)


def append_audit_trail(mbo_rec, audit_text):
    """Append audit_text to the ASSETMETER longdescription for the AUDIT meter."""
    try:
        meter_set = mbo_rec.getMboSet("ASSETMETER")
        meter_set.setWhere("METERNAME = '%s'" % AUDIT_METER_NAME)
        meter_set.reset()
        meter_rec = meter_set.moveFirst()

        if meter_rec is None:
            logger.debug("No AUDIT meter found for asset %s; skipping audit trail."
                         % mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM"))
            return

        existing = meter_rec.getString("DESCRIPTION_LONGDESCRIPTION")
        if existing is None:
            existing = ""

        timestamp = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss").format(Date())
        user_id = mbo_rec.getUserInfo().getUserName()
        entry = "[%s | %s] %s" % (timestamp, user_id, audit_text)

        # Prepend new entry so most recent is on top
        new_text = entry + "\n" + existing
        meter_rec.setValue("DESCRIPTION_LONGDESCRIPTION", new_text,
                           MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK | MboConstants.NOVALIDATION)
        logger.debug("Audit trail updated for asset %s" % mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM"))

    except Exception, ex:
        logger.error("Failed to append audit trail for asset %s: %s"
                     % (mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM"), str(ex)))


# ==============================================================================
# Rule 1: Validate INSTALLDATE when STATUS changes to OPERATING
# ==============================================================================
def validate_operating_status(mbo_rec):
    """
    If STATUS is changing to OPERATING, INSTALLDATE must be populated.
    Raises MXApplicationException on violation.
    """
    if not mbo_rec.getMboValueData("STATUS").isModified():
        return

    new_status = mbo_rec.getString("STATUS")
    if new_status is None:
        return

    if new_status.upper() == "OPERATING":
        install_date = mbo_rec.getDate("INSTALLDATE")
        if install_date is None:
            from psdi.util import MXApplicationException
            raise MXApplicationException("asset", "installDateRequired",
                [mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM")])


# ==============================================================================
# Rule 2: Auto-calculate REPLACECOST via straight-line depreciation
# ==============================================================================
def calculate_replacement_cost(mbo_rec):
    """
    REPLACECOST = PURCHASEPRICE * (1 - age / USEFUL_LIFE_YEARS)
    Floors at MIN_REPLACE_COST.  Only recalculates when PURCHASEPRICE or
    INSTALLDATE has been modified, or when REPLACECOST is currently null/zero.
    """
    purchase_modified = mbo_rec.getMboValueData("PURCHASEPRICE").isModified()
    install_modified  = mbo_rec.getMboValueData("INSTALLDATE").isModified()
    current_cost      = mbo_rec.getDouble("REPLACECOST")

    if not (purchase_modified or install_modified or current_cost == 0.0):
        return

    purchase_price = mbo_rec.getDouble("PURCHASEPRICE")
    if purchase_price <= 0:
        return

    install_date = mbo_rec.getDate("INSTALLDATE")
    if install_date is None:
        # Cannot calculate without an install date; leave as-is
        return

    age_years = get_years_since(install_date)
    depreciation_factor = max(1.0 - (float(age_years) / float(USEFUL_LIFE_YEARS)), 0.0)
    replace_cost = round(purchase_price * depreciation_factor, 2)
    replace_cost = max(replace_cost, MIN_REPLACE_COST)

    mbo_rec.setValue("REPLACECOST", replace_cost,
                     MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK | MboConstants.NOVALIDATION)
    logger.debug("REPLACECOST calculated for asset %s: $%.2f (age=%d yrs, factor=%.4f)"
                 % (mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM"), replace_cost,
                    age_years, depreciation_factor))


# ==============================================================================
# Rule 3: Sync LOCATION with parent asset when PARENT changes
# ==============================================================================
def sync_location_with_parent(mbo_rec):
    """
    When PARENT changes, update this asset's LOCATION to match the parent's LOCATION.
    """
    if not mbo_rec.getMboValueData("PARENT").isModified():
        return

    parent_assetnum = mbo_rec.getString("PARENT")
    if parent_assetnum is None or parent_assetnum.strip() == "":
        logger.debug("PARENT cleared on asset %s; LOCATION unchanged."
                     % mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM"))
        return

    try:
        parent_set = mbo_rec.getMboSet("$PARENTASSET", "ASSET",
                                        "ASSETNUM = '%s' AND SITEID = '%s'"
                                        % (parent_assetnum, mbo_rec.getString("SITEID")))
        parent_rec = parent_set.moveFirst()

        if parent_rec is not None:
            parent_location = parent_rec.getString("LOCATION")
            if parent_location is not None and parent_location.strip() != "":
                mbo_rec.setValue("LOCATION", parent_location,
                                MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK | MboConstants.NOVALIDATION)
                logger.debug("LOCATION updated to '%s' from parent asset '%s'"
                             % (parent_location, parent_assetnum))
            else:
                logger.debug("Parent asset '%s' has no LOCATION; skipping sync."
                             % parent_assetnum)
        else:
            logger.warn("Parent asset '%s' not found for asset '%s'."
                        % (parent_assetnum, mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM")))

    except Exception, ex:
        logger.error("Error syncing location from parent: %s" % str(ex))


# ==============================================================================
# Rule 4: Require SERIALNUM when ASSETTYPE is "FLEET"
# ==============================================================================
def validate_fleet_serial(mbo_rec):
    """
    If ASSETTYPE is FLEET, SERIALNUM must be populated.
    Raises MXApplicationException on violation.
    """
    asset_type = mbo_rec.getString("ASSETTYPE")
    if asset_type is None:
        return

    if asset_type.upper() == "FLEET":
        serial = mbo_rec.getString("SERIALNUM")
        if serial is None or serial.strip() == "":
            from psdi.util import MXApplicationException
            raise MXApplicationException("asset", "serialRequiredForFleet",
                [mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM")])


# ==============================================================================
# Rule 5: Log field changes to audit trail
# ==============================================================================
def log_field_changes(mbo_rec):
    """
    Inspect a predefined list of important fields.  If any have been modified,
    build a consolidated change log entry and append it to the ASSETMETER
    audit trail.
    """
    tracked_fields = [
        "STATUS", "INSTALLDATE", "PURCHASEPRICE", "REPLACECOST",
        "PARENT", "LOCATION", "ASSETTYPE", "SERIALNUM",
        "DESCRIPTION", "PRIORITY", "FAILURECODE"
    ]

    changes = []
    for field in tracked_fields:
        change = get_field_change_text(mbo_rec, field)
        if change is not None:
            changes.append(change)

    if len(changes) > 0:
        audit_text = "Fields modified: " + " | ".join(changes)
        append_audit_trail(mbo_rec, audit_text)
        logger.debug("Audit logged %d field change(s) for asset %s"
                     % (len(changes), mbo_rec.getString("ASSETNUM")))


# ==============================================================================
# Main Execution
# ==============================================================================
try:
    logger.debug("ASSET_SAVE script executing for asset: %s"
                 % mbo.getString("ASSETNUM"))

    # --- Validations (run first; will throw exceptions to block save) ---
    validate_operating_status(mbo)
    validate_fleet_serial(mbo)

    # --- Auto-population ---
    calculate_replacement_cost(mbo)
    sync_location_with_parent(mbo)

    # --- Audit Trail ---
    log_field_changes(mbo)

    logger.debug("ASSET_SAVE script completed successfully for asset: %s"
                 % mbo.getString("ASSETNUM"))

except Exception, ex:
    # Re-raise MXApplicationExceptions so Maximo displays them to the user
    from psdi.util import MXApplicationException
    if isinstance(ex, MXApplicationException):
        raise
    logger.error("Unexpected error in ASSET_SAVE script: %s" % str(ex))
    raise MXApplicationException("asset", "scriptError", [str(ex)])


# ==============================================================================
# scriptConfig — Launch Point & Script Registration
# ==============================================================================
scriptConfig = {
    "autoscript": "ASSET.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
    "description": "Validate install date, calculate depreciation, sync parent location, audit trail",
    "version": "1.0",
    "active": True,
    "logLevel": "ERROR",
    "scriptLaunchPoints": [
        {
            "launchPointName": "ASSET_BEFORESAVE",
            "launchPointType": "OBJECT",
            "objectName": "ASSET",
            "save": True,
            "add": True,
            "update": True,
            "delete": False,
            "beforeSave": True,
            "active": True
        }
    ]
}
",
  "status": "Active",
  "version": "1.0",
  "loglevel": "ERROR",
  "scriptlaunchpoint": [
    {
      "launchpointname": "ASSET_BEFORESAVE",
      "launchpointtype": "OBJECT",
      "active": true,
      "autoscript": "ASSET.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
      "objectname": "ASSET",
      "add": true,
      "update": true,
      "beforesave": true
    }
  ]
}

body.raw:
{
  "autoscript": "LOCATIONS.OBJ.INIT",
  "description": "Auto-generate hierarchy path, inherit parent attributes, default GL account on new location",
  "scriptlanguage": "jython",
  "active": true,
  "source": "# ==============================================================================
# Script Name:      locations.obj.init
# Description:      Initializes new LOCATIONS records with defaults.
#                   Auto-generates hierarchy path in long description,
#                   defaults ORGID/SITEID from user insert site, inherits
#                   key fields from parent location, sets default GLACCOUNT
#                   based on TYPE, and auto-sets STATUS to OPERATING.
# Object:           LOCATIONS
# Launch Point Type: Object Launch Point
# Event:            Add (initialize on new record)
# Variables:
#   &mbo           - Implicit. Current LOCATIONS MBO being initialized.
#   &mboSet        - Implicit. The MboSet containing the current MBO.
#   &onAdd         - Implicit. True when the MBO is being added.
# Usage Notes:
#   - Attach this script to an Object Launch Point on LOCATIONS with the
#     "Add" (Initialize Value) event.
#   - The GL_ACCOUNT_BY_TYPE lookup table should be customized to match your
#     organization's chart of accounts structure.
#   - Parent field inheritance only fires when PARENT is already set at add
#     time (e.g., when adding from a hierarchy view). For runtime parent
#     changes, use a separate attribute launch point on PARENT.
#   - The hierarchy path builder walks up to 20 levels to prevent infinite
#     loops from misconfigured circular parent references.
# ==============================================================================

from psdi.mbo import MboConstants
from psdi.util.logging import MXLoggerFactory
from psdi.server import MXServer

logger = MXLoggerFactory.getLogger("autoscript.LOCATIONS_INIT")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GL Account lookup table keyed by location TYPE.
# Customize these values to match your organization's chart of accounts.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
GL_ACCOUNT_BY_TYPE = {
    "OPERATING":  "6000-200-000",
    "REPAIR":     "6000-300-000",
    "STOREROOM":  "6100-100-000",
    "COURIER":    "6200-100-000",
    "LABOR":      "6000-400-000",
    "VENDOR":     "6200-200-000",
}
DEFAULT_GL_ACCOUNT = "6000-000-000"

# Maximum parent chain depth to prevent infinite loops
MAX_HIERARCHY_DEPTH = 20


def build_hierarchy_path(location_mbo):
    """
    Walk the parent chain from the given location upward and return a
    human-readable hierarchy path string.

    Example result: "SITE > BUILDING-A > FLOOR-2 > ROOM-201"

    Returns None if the location has no parent (path is just itself).
    """
    path_parts = []
    current = location_mbo
    depth = 0

    while current is not None and depth < MAX_HIERARCHY_DEPTH:
        loc_value = current.getString("LOCATION")
        if loc_value is not None and loc_value.strip() != "":
            path_parts.insert(0, loc_value.strip())
        else:
            break

        # Move to parent
        parent_value = current.getString("PARENT")
        if parent_value is None or parent_value.strip() == "":
            break

        orgid = current.getString("ORGID")
        siteid = current.getString("SITEID")

        parent_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("LOCATIONS", current.getUserInfo())
        parent_set.setWhere(
            "location = '%s' and siteid = '%s'" % (parent_value.strip(), siteid)
        )
        parent_set.reset()
        current = parent_set.moveFirst()
        depth += 1

    if len(path_parts) <= 1:
        return None

    return " > ".join(path_parts)


def set_default_org_site(location_mbo):
    """
    Set ORGID and SITEID from the user's default insert site if they
    are not already populated.
    """
    changes = []

    if location_mbo.isNull("SITEID"):
        user_info = location_mbo.getUserInfo()
        default_site = user_info.getInsertSite()
        if default_site is not None and default_site.strip() != "":
            location_mbo.setValue("SITEID", default_site, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
            changes.append("SITEID set to '%s'" % default_site)

            # Derive ORGID from the site
            site_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("SITE", user_info)
            site_set.setWhere("siteid = '%s'" % default_site)
            site_set.reset()
            site_mbo = site_set.moveFirst()
            if site_mbo is not None:
                orgid = site_mbo.getString("ORGID")
                location_mbo.setValue("ORGID", orgid, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
                changes.append("ORGID set to '%s'" % orgid)

    elif location_mbo.isNull("ORGID"):
        # SITEID is set but ORGID is not -- derive it
        siteid = location_mbo.getString("SITEID")
        user_info = location_mbo.getUserInfo()
        site_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("SITE", user_info)
        site_set.setWhere("siteid = '%s'" % siteid)
        site_set.reset()
        site_mbo = site_set.moveFirst()
        if site_mbo is not None:
            orgid = site_mbo.getString("ORGID")
            location_mbo.setValue("ORGID", orgid, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
            changes.append("ORGID derived as '%s' from SITEID '%s'" % (orgid, siteid))

    return changes


def inherit_from_parent(location_mbo):
    """
    If PARENT is specified, look up the parent LOCATIONS record and
    inherit TYPE, LOCPRIORITY, and FAILURECODE from it.
    """
    changes = []
    parent_value = location_mbo.getString("PARENT")

    if parent_value is None or parent_value.strip() == "":
        return changes

    siteid = location_mbo.getString("SITEID")
    if siteid is None or siteid.strip() == "":
        logger.debug("LOCATIONS_INIT: Cannot inherit from parent -- SITEID not set.")
        return changes

    parent_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("LOCATIONS", location_mbo.getUserInfo())
    parent_set.setWhere(
        "location = '%s' and siteid = '%s'" % (parent_value.strip(), siteid)
    )
    parent_set.reset()
    parent_mbo = parent_set.moveFirst()

    if parent_mbo is None:
        logger.warn(
            "LOCATIONS_INIT: Parent location '%s' not found in site '%s'."
            % (parent_value, siteid)
        )
        return changes

    # Inherit TYPE
    if location_mbo.isNull("TYPE") and not parent_mbo.isNull("TYPE"):
        parent_type = parent_mbo.getString("TYPE")
        location_mbo.setValue("TYPE", parent_type, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
        changes.append("TYPE inherited as '%s'" % parent_type)

    # Inherit LOCPRIORITY
    if location_mbo.isNull("LOCPRIORITY") and not parent_mbo.isNull("LOCPRIORITY"):
        parent_priority = parent_mbo.getInt("LOCPRIORITY")
        location_mbo.setValue("LOCPRIORITY", parent_priority, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
        changes.append("LOCPRIORITY inherited as %d" % parent_priority)

    # Inherit FAILURECODE
    if location_mbo.isNull("FAILURECODE") and not parent_mbo.isNull("FAILURECODE"):
        parent_fc = parent_mbo.getString("FAILURECODE")
        location_mbo.setValue("FAILURECODE", parent_fc, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
        changes.append("FAILURECODE inherited as '%s'" % parent_fc)

    return changes


def set_default_glaccount(location_mbo):
    """
    Set GLACCOUNT based on the location's TYPE value using the
    GL_ACCOUNT_BY_TYPE lookup table.
    """
    if not location_mbo.isNull("GLACCOUNT"):
        return None

    loc_type = location_mbo.getString("TYPE")
    if loc_type is not None and loc_type.strip() != "":
        gl = GL_ACCOUNT_BY_TYPE.get(loc_type.strip().upper(), DEFAULT_GL_ACCOUNT)
    else:
        gl = DEFAULT_GL_ACCOUNT

    location_mbo.setValue("GLACCOUNT", gl, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
    return "GLACCOUNT set to '%s' (type=%s)" % (gl, loc_type)


def set_default_status(location_mbo):
    """Set STATUS to OPERATING for new locations if not already set."""
    if location_mbo.isNull("STATUS"):
        location_mbo.setValue("STATUS", "OPERATING", MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)
        return "STATUS set to OPERATING"
    return None


def set_hierarchy_path(location_mbo):
    """
    Build the location hierarchy path and store it in the long
    description field for reference.
    """
    path = build_hierarchy_path(location_mbo)
    if path is not None:
        location_mbo.setValue(
            "DESCRIPTION_LONGDESCRIPTION",
            "Location Hierarchy: %s" % path,
            MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK
        )
        return "Hierarchy path set: %s" % path
    return None


def initialize_location(location_mbo):
    """
    Main initialization logic for a new LOCATIONS record.
    Orchestrates all defaulting and inheritance rules.
    """
    location = location_mbo.getString("LOCATION")
    all_changes = []

    # Step 1: Default ORGID and SITEID
    org_site_changes = set_default_org_site(location_mbo)
    all_changes.extend(org_site_changes)

    # Step 2: Inherit fields from parent
    parent_changes = inherit_from_parent(location_mbo)
    all_changes.extend(parent_changes)

    # Step 3: Default GLACCOUNT based on TYPE
    gl_change = set_default_glaccount(location_mbo)
    if gl_change:
        all_changes.append(gl_change)

    # Step 4: Default STATUS to OPERATING
    status_change = set_default_status(location_mbo)
    if status_change:
        all_changes.append(status_change)

    # Step 5: Build hierarchy path in long description
    path_change = set_hierarchy_path(location_mbo)
    if path_change:
        all_changes.append(path_change)

    # Summary log
    if all_changes:
        logger.info(
            "LOCATIONS_INIT: [%s] Initialized with %d default(s): %s"
            % (location, len(all_changes), "; ".join(all_changes))
        )
    else:
        logger.debug("LOCATIONS_INIT: [%s] No defaults applied." % location)


# ==============================================================================
# Main execution
# ==============================================================================
try:
    initialize_location(mbo)
except:
    import sys
    logger.error(
        "LOCATIONS_INIT: Unexpected error during initialization: %s"
        % str(sys.exc_info()[1])
    )
    raise

# ==============================================================================
# scriptConfig — Launch Point & Script Registration
# ==============================================================================
scriptConfig = {
    "autoscript": "LOCATIONS.OBJ.INIT",
    "description": "Auto-generate hierarchy path, inherit parent attributes, default GL account on new location",
    "version": "1.0",
    "active": True,
    "logLevel": "ERROR",
    "scriptLaunchPoints": [
        {
            "launchPointName": "LOCATIONS_INIT",
            "launchPointType": "OBJECT",
            "objectName": "LOCATIONS",
            "initializeValue": True,
            "active": True
        }
    ]
}

      "autoscript": "LOCATIONS.OBJ.INIT",

{
  "autoscript": "PM.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
  "description": "Validate PM frequency/job plan, calculate NEXTDATE/EXTDATE, detect WO overlap",
  "scriptlanguage": "jython",
  "active": true,
  "source": "# ==============================================================================
# Script Name:      pm.obj.save.before
# Description:      Validates and auto-calculates PM scheduling on save.
#                   Ensures FREQUENCY/FREQUNIT are set for active PMs,
#                   verifies job plan existence, calculates NEXTDATE and
#                   EXTDATE based on frequency and lead time, prevents
#                   overlapping work order generation, and logs all
#                   scheduling calculations for audit.
# Object:           PM
# Launch Point Type: Object Launch Point
# Event:            Save (before save)
# Variables:
#   &mbo           - Implicit. Current PM MBO being saved.
#   &mboSet        - Implicit. The MboSet containing the current MBO.
# Usage Notes:
#   - Attach to an Object Launch Point on PM with "Before Save" event.
#   - NEXTDATE calculation uses java.util.Calendar for date arithmetic.
#   - The overlap check compares NEXTDATE against the last generated work
#     order's TARGCOMPDATE. Adjust the WORKORDER query relationship if your
#     environment uses a different linking mechanism.
#   - Lead time (LEADTIME) is expected in days. EXTDATE = NEXTDATE - LEADTIME.
#   - All scheduling calculations are logged at INFO level for audit trail.
# ==============================================================================

from psdi.mbo import MboConstants
from psdi.util.logging import MXLoggerFactory
from psdi.util import MXApplicationException
from psdi.server import MXServer
from java.util import Calendar, Date

logger = MXLoggerFactory.getLogger("autoscript.PM_SAVE")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Frequency unit multipliers (convert to Calendar field + multiplier)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FREQ_UNIT_MAP = {
    "DAYS":   Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR,
    "WEEKS":  Calendar.WEEK_OF_YEAR,
    "MONTHS": Calendar.MONTH,
    "YEARS":  Calendar.YEAR,
}


def validate_frequency_required(pm_mbo):
    """
    Active PMs must have both FREQUENCY and FREQUNIT set.
    Raises MXApplicationException if either is missing.
    """
    status = pm_mbo.getString("STATUS")
    if status is None or status.strip().upper() != "ACTIVE":
        return

    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    errors = []

    if pm_mbo.isNull("FREQUENCY") or pm_mbo.getInt("FREQUENCY") <= 0:
        errors.append("FREQUENCY")

    if pm_mbo.isNull("FREQUNIT"):
        errors.append("FREQUNIT")
    else:
        frequnit = pm_mbo.getString("FREQUNIT")
        if frequnit is None or frequnit.strip() == "":
            errors.append("FREQUNIT")

    if errors:
        logger.warn(
            "PM_SAVE: [%s] Active PM missing required scheduling fields: %s"
            % (pmnum, ", ".join(errors))
        )
        raise MXApplicationException(
            "pm", "missingfrequency",
            [pmnum, ", ".join(errors)]
        )

    logger.debug("PM_SAVE: [%s] Frequency validation passed." % pmnum)


def validate_job_plan(pm_mbo):
    """
    If JPNUM (job plan number) is specified, verify that the job plan
    exists and is in ACTIVE status.
    """
    if pm_mbo.isNull("JPNUM"):
        return

    jpnum = pm_mbo.getString("JPNUM")
    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    siteid = pm_mbo.getString("SITEID")
    orgid = pm_mbo.getString("ORGID")

    if jpnum is None or jpnum.strip() == "":
        return

    jp_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("JOBPLAN", pm_mbo.getUserInfo())

    # Job plans can be site-level or org-level
    where_clause = "jpnum = '%s'" % jpnum.strip()
    if siteid is not None and siteid.strip() != "":
        where_clause += " and (siteid = '%s' or siteid is null)" % siteid
    if orgid is not None and orgid.strip() != "":
        where_clause += " and orgid = '%s'" % orgid

    jp_set.setWhere(where_clause)
    jp_set.reset()
    jp_mbo = jp_set.moveFirst()

    if jp_mbo is None:
        logger.warn(
            "PM_SAVE: [%s] Job plan '%s' not found." % (pmnum, jpnum)
        )
        raise MXApplicationException(
            "pm", "jpnotfound",
            [pmnum, jpnum]
        )

    jp_status = jp_mbo.getString("STATUS")
    if jp_status is not None and jp_status.strip().upper() != "ACTIVE":
        logger.warn(
            "PM_SAVE: [%s] Job plan '%s' is not ACTIVE (status=%s)."
            % (pmnum, jpnum, jp_status)
        )
        raise MXApplicationException(
            "pm", "jpnotactive",
            [pmnum, jpnum, jp_status]
        )

    logger.debug("PM_SAVE: [%s] Job plan '%s' validated (ACTIVE)." % (pmnum, jpnum))


def get_last_completion_date(pm_mbo):
    """
    Retrieve the most recent completion date from work orders generated
    by this PM. Returns a java.util.Date or None if no completed WOs exist.
    """
    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    siteid = pm_mbo.getString("SITEID")

    wo_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("WORKORDER", pm_mbo.getUserInfo())
    where_clause = (
        "pmnum = '%s' and siteid = '%s' and status in ('COMP', 'CLOSE') "
        "and actfinish is not null"
        % (pmnum, siteid)
    )
    wo_set.setWhere(where_clause)
    wo_set.setOrderBy("actfinish desc")
    wo_set.reset()

    last_wo = wo_set.moveFirst()
    if last_wo is not None:
        return last_wo.getDate("ACTFINISH")

    return None


def calculate_next_date(pm_mbo):
    """
    Calculate NEXTDATE based on FREQUENCY, FREQUNIT, and the last
    completion date (or today if no completions exist).

    Sets NEXTDATE on the PM record and logs the calculation.
    """
    if pm_mbo.isNull("FREQUENCY") or pm_mbo.isNull("FREQUNIT"):
        return None

    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    frequency = pm_mbo.getInt("FREQUENCY")
    frequnit = pm_mbo.getString("FREQUNIT").strip().upper()

    if frequnit not in FREQ_UNIT_MAP:
        logger.warn(
            "PM_SAVE: [%s] Unknown FREQUNIT '%s' -- skipping NEXTDATE calculation."
            % (pmnum, frequnit)
        )
        return None

    # Determine the base date: last completion or today
    base_date = get_last_completion_date(pm_mbo)
    base_source = "last completion"

    if base_date is None:
        base_date = Date()
        base_source = "today (no prior completions)"

    # Calculate next date
    cal = Calendar.getInstance()
    cal.setTime(base_date)
    cal.add(FREQ_UNIT_MAP[frequnit], frequency)
    next_date = cal.getTime()

    pm_mbo.setValue("NEXTDATE", next_date, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)

    logger.info(
        "PM_SAVE: [%s] NEXTDATE calculated: %s (base=%s [%s], frequency=%d %s)"
        % (pmnum, str(next_date), str(base_date), base_source, frequency, frequnit)
    )

    return next_date


def calculate_ext_date(pm_mbo, next_date):
    """
    If LEADTIME is set, calculate EXTDATE as NEXTDATE minus LEADTIME days.
    EXTDATE represents the date by which materials/resources should be
    staged for the upcoming PM work order.
    """
    if next_date is None:
        return None

    if pm_mbo.isNull("LEADTIME") or pm_mbo.getInt("LEADTIME") <= 0:
        return None

    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    lead_time = pm_mbo.getInt("LEADTIME")

    cal = Calendar.getInstance()
    cal.setTime(next_date)
    cal.add(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR, -lead_time)
    ext_date = cal.getTime()

    pm_mbo.setValue("EXTDATE", ext_date, MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK)

    logger.info(
        "PM_SAVE: [%s] EXTDATE calculated: %s (NEXTDATE %s minus %d day lead time)"
        % (pmnum, str(ext_date), str(next_date), lead_time)
    )

    return ext_date


def check_wo_overlap(pm_mbo, next_date):
    """
    Prevent saving if NEXTDATE falls before the last generated work
    order's target completion date, which would cause overlapping PMs.
    """
    if next_date is None:
        return

    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    siteid = pm_mbo.getString("SITEID")

    # Find the most recent non-completed work order for this PM
    wo_set = MXServer.getMXServer().getMboSet("WORKORDER", pm_mbo.getUserInfo())
    where_clause = (
        "pmnum = '%s' and siteid = '%s' "
        "and status not in ('COMP', 'CLOSE', 'CAN') "
        "and targcompdate is not null"
        % (pmnum, siteid)
    )
    wo_set.setWhere(where_clause)
    wo_set.setOrderBy("targcompdate desc")
    wo_set.reset()

    last_wo = wo_set.moveFirst()
    if last_wo is None:
        logger.debug("PM_SAVE: [%s] No open work orders -- no overlap possible." % pmnum)
        return

    targ_comp_date = last_wo.getDate("TARGCOMPDATE")
    if targ_comp_date is None:
        return

    if next_date.before(targ_comp_date):
        wonum = last_wo.getString("WONUM")
        logger.warn(
            "PM_SAVE: [%s] Overlap detected -- NEXTDATE %s is before WO %s TARGCOMPDATE %s."
            % (pmnum, str(next_date), wonum, str(targ_comp_date))
        )
        raise MXApplicationException(
            "pm", "wooverlap",
            [pmnum, str(next_date), wonum, str(targ_comp_date)]
        )

    logger.debug("PM_SAVE: [%s] No work order overlap detected." % pmnum)


def process_pm_save(pm_mbo):
    """
    Main save logic for PM records. Orchestrates validation, date
    calculations, and overlap checks.
    """
    pmnum = pm_mbo.getString("PMNUM")
    status = pm_mbo.getString("STATUS")

    logger.info("PM_SAVE: [%s] Processing save (status=%s)." % (pmnum, status))

    # Step 1: Validate frequency fields for active PMs
    validate_frequency_required(pm_mbo)

    # Step 2: Validate job plan if specified
    validate_job_plan(pm_mbo)

    # Step 3: Calculate NEXTDATE
    next_date = calculate_next_date(pm_mbo)

    # Step 4: Calculate EXTDATE if lead time is configured
    ext_date = calculate_ext_date(pm_mbo, next_date)

    # Step 5: Check for work order overlap
    check_wo_overlap(pm_mbo, next_date)

    # Audit summary
    logger.info(
        "PM_SAVE: [%s] Save processing complete. NEXTDATE=%s, EXTDATE=%s"
        % (
            pmnum,
            str(next_date) if next_date else "unchanged",
            str(ext_date) if ext_date else "not set"
        )
    )


# ==============================================================================
# Main execution
# ==============================================================================
try:
    process_pm_save(mbo)
except:
    import sys
    exc = sys.exc_info()[1]
    # Re-raise MXApplicationException as-is so Maximo shows the proper error
    if isinstance(exc, MXApplicationException):
        raise
    logger.error(
        "PM_SAVE: Unexpected error during PM save processing: %s" % str(exc)
    )
    raise

# ==============================================================================
# scriptConfig — Launch Point & Script Registration
# ==============================================================================
scriptConfig = {
    "autoscript": "PM.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
    "description": "Validate PM frequency/job plan, calculate NEXTDATE/EXTDATE, detect WO overlap",
    "version": "1.0",
    "active": True,
    "logLevel": "ERROR",
    "scriptLaunchPoints": [
        {
            "launchPointName": "PM_BEFORESAVE",
            "launchPointType": "OBJECT",
            "objectName": "PM",
            "save": True,
            "add": True,
            "update": True,
            "delete": False,
            "beforeSave": True,
            "active": True
        }
    ]
}
",
  "status": "Active",
  "version": "1.0",
  "loglevel": "ERROR",
  "scriptlaunchpoint": [
    {
      "launchpointname": "PM_BEFORESAVE",
      "launchpointtype": "OBJECT",
      "active": true,
      "autoscript": "PM.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
      "objectname": "PM",
      "add": true,
      "update": true,
      "beforesave": true
    }
  ]
}

body.raw:
{
  "autoscript": "PO.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
  "description": "Calculate PO total cost, validate vendor, check approval threshold, validate line items",
  "scriptlanguage": "jython",
  "active": true,
  "source": """"
===============================================================================
Script Name:    po.obj.save.before
Description:    Purchase Order before-save processing. Calculates total cost
                from PO lines, validates vendor status, checks approval
                thresholds, populates currency defaults, validates line items,
                and increments revision number on status changes.

Object:         PO
Launch Point:   Object Launch Point
Event:          Before Save
Active:         Yes

Variables:      (None - uses MAXVARS for configuration)

MAXVAR Dependencies:
    POAPPROVALLIMIT  - Dollar threshold above which PO requires approval

Usage Notes:
    - Attach to PO object via Object Launch Point (Before Save)
    - Calculates TOTALCOST as SUM(ORDERQTY * UNITCOST + TAX) across POLINE
    - Validates VENDOR is active and not on hold via COMPANIES table
    - Sets POAPPROVAL flag when TOTALCOST exceeds POAPPROVALLIMIT MAXVAR
    - Auto-populates CURRENCYCODE and EXCHANGERATE from vendor defaults
    - Prevents save if any POLINE references an inactive ITEM
    - Increments REVISIONNUM when PO is in WAPPR or APPR status

Implicit Variables:
    mbo         - Current PO MBO being saved
    mboSet      - Parent MboSet (not used directly)
    service     - Service reference for error handling
    interactive - True if running in UI context
    user        - Current user login ID
    userInfo    - Current user's UserInfo object

Launch Point Configuration:
    1. Go to System Configuration > Automation Scripts
    2. Create Script: po.obj.save.before
    3. Add Object Launch Point:
       - Object: PO
       - Event: Before Save
       - Active: Yes

Change History:
    2026-03-01  Initial version - generic demo library
===============================================================================
"""

from psdi.mbo import MboConstants
from psdi.server import MXServer
from psdi.util.logging import MXLoggerFactory

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logger setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
logger = MXLoggerFactory.getLogger("autoscript.po.obj.save.before")

NOACCESSCHECK = MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK
NOVALIDATION = MboConstants.NOVALIDATION
NOACTION = MboConstants.NOACTION
FLAGS = NOACCESSCHECK | NOVALIDATION


def calculate_total_cost(poMbo):
    """
    Calculate PO total cost from all POLINE records.
    Formula per line: ORDERQTY * UNITCOST + TAX
    Returns the computed total as a float.
    """
    totalCost = 0.0
    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()

    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()
    while poLine is not None:
        orderQty = poLine.getDouble("ORDERQTY") if not poLine.isNull("ORDERQTY") else 0.0
        unitCost = poLine.getDouble("UNITCOST") if not poLine.isNull("UNITCOST") else 0.0
        tax = poLine.getDouble("TAX1") if not poLine.isNull("TAX1") else 0.0
        lineCost = (orderQty * unitCost) + tax
        totalCost += lineCost
        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    logger.debug("Calculated PO total cost: " + str(totalCost))
    return totalCost


def validate_vendor_active(poMbo):
    """
    Validate that the vendor on the PO is active and not on hold.
    Raises a service error if vendor is inactive or on hold.
    Returns True if valid, False otherwise.
    """
    if poMbo.isNull("VENDOR"):
        return True  # No vendor set yet, skip validation

    vendor = poMbo.getString("VENDOR")
    orgId = poMbo.getString("ORGID")
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    companySet = mxServer.getMboSet("COMPANIES", poMbo.getUserInfo())
    companySet.setWhere(
        "company = '" + vendor + "' and orgid = '" + orgId + "'"
    )
    companySet.reset()

    if companySet.isEmpty():
        service.error("po", "vendornotfound")
        return False

    companyMbo = companySet.getMbo(0)

    # Check if vendor is disabled
    if companyMbo.getBoolean("DISABLED"):
        service.error("po", "vendorinactive")
        return False

    # Check if vendor is on hold (payment hold or purchase hold)
    if not companyMbo.isNull("PAYMENTHOLD"):
        payHold = companyMbo.getString("PAYMENTHOLD")
        if payHold is not None and len(payHold.strip()) > 0:
            service.error("po", "vendoronhold")
            return False

    logger.debug("Vendor " + vendor + " validated successfully")
    return True


def check_approval_threshold(poMbo, totalCost):
    """
    Check if the PO total cost exceeds the approval threshold defined
    in MAXVARS (POAPPROVALLIMIT). If it does, set the POAPPROVAL flag.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    try:
        approvalLimit = mxServer.getMaxVar("POAPPROVALLIMIT", poMbo.getString("ORGID"))
        if approvalLimit is not None and len(approvalLimit.strip()) > 0:
            threshold = float(approvalLimit)
            if totalCost > threshold:
                poMbo.setValue("POAPPROVAL", 1, FLAGS)
                logger.info(
                    "PO total cost " + str(totalCost) +
                    " exceeds approval limit " + str(threshold) +
                    ". POAPPROVAL flag set."
                )
            else:
                poMbo.setValue("POAPPROVAL", 0, FLAGS)
        else:
            logger.debug("POAPPROVALLIMIT MAXVAR not configured; skipping threshold check")
    except Exception, ex:
        logger.warn(
            "Could not retrieve POAPPROVALLIMIT MAXVAR: " + str(ex) +
            ". Skipping approval threshold check."
        )


def populate_currency_from_vendor(poMbo):
    """
    Auto-populate CURRENCYCODE and EXCHANGERATE from vendor defaults
    in the COMPANIES table. Only sets values if they are not already
    populated on the PO.
    """
    if poMbo.isNull("VENDOR"):
        return

    vendor = poMbo.getString("VENDOR")
    orgId = poMbo.getString("ORGID")
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()

    companySet = mxServer.getMboSet("COMPANIES", poMbo.getUserInfo())
    companySet.setWhere(
        "company = '" + vendor + "' and orgid = '" + orgId + "'"
    )
    companySet.reset()

    if companySet.isEmpty():
        return

    companyMbo = companySet.getMbo(0)

    # Set currency code from vendor if PO currency is blank
    if poMbo.isNull("CURRENCYCODE"):
        if not companyMbo.isNull("CURRENCYCODE"):
            vendorCurrency = companyMbo.getString("CURRENCYCODE")
            poMbo.setValue("CURRENCYCODE", vendorCurrency, FLAGS)
            logger.debug("Set CURRENCYCODE to " + vendorCurrency + " from vendor defaults")

    # Set exchange rate if not already set
    if poMbo.isNull("EXCHANGERATE") or poMbo.getDouble("EXCHANGERATE") == 0.0:
        if not companyMbo.isNull("EXCHANGERATE"):
            vendorRate = companyMbo.getDouble("EXCHANGERATE")
            if vendorRate > 0.0:
                poMbo.setValue("EXCHANGERATE", vendorRate, FLAGS)
                logger.debug("Set EXCHANGERATE to " + str(vendorRate) + " from vendor defaults")


def validate_line_items_active(poMbo):
    """
    Validate that all POLINE records reference active items in the ITEM table.
    Prevents saving if any line has an inactive item.
    Returns True if all valid, False otherwise.
    """
    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()

    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()
    while poLine is not None:
        if not poLine.isNull("ITEMNUM"):
            itemNum = poLine.getString("ITEMNUM")
            itemSetId = poLine.getString("ITEMSETID") if not poLine.isNull("ITEMSETID") else ""

            itemSet = mxServer.getMboSet("ITEM", poMbo.getUserInfo())
            whereClause = "itemnum = '" + itemNum + "'"
            if itemSetId:
                whereClause += " and itemsetid = '" + itemSetId + "'"
            itemSet.setWhere(whereClause)
            itemSet.reset()

            if not itemSet.isEmpty():
                itemMbo = itemSet.getMbo(0)
                status = itemMbo.getString("STATUS") if not itemMbo.isNull("STATUS") else ""
                if status.upper() in ("INACTIVE", "OBSOLETE", "PENDING"):
                    lineNum = str(poLine.getInt("POLINENUM"))
                    logger.error(
                        "POLINE " + lineNum + " references inactive item " +
                        itemNum + " (status: " + status + ")"
                    )
                    service.error("po", "inactiveitem")
                    return False
        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    logger.debug("All PO line items validated as active")
    return True


def increment_revision(poMbo):
    """
    Increment REVISIONNUM if the PO is in WAPPR (Waiting for Approval)
    or APPR (Approved) status. This tracks revision history for
    audit purposes.
    """
    if poMbo.isNull("STATUS"):
        return

    status = poMbo.getString("STATUS").upper()
    if status in ("WAPPR", "APPR"):
        currentRev = 0
        if not poMbo.isNull("REVISIONNUM"):
            currentRev = poMbo.getInt("REVISIONNUM")
        newRev = currentRev + 1
        poMbo.setValue("REVISIONNUM", newRev, FLAGS)
        logger.info(
            "PO revision incremented from " + str(currentRev) +
            " to " + str(newRev) + " (status: " + status + ")"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION
# ===========================================================================

try:
    logger.debug("PO before-save script starting for PO: " +
                 (mbo.getString("PONUM") if not mbo.isNull("PONUM") else "NEW"))

    # Step 1: Validate vendor is active and not on hold
    if not validate_vendor_active(mbo):
        # Error already raised inside the function; script stops here
        pass
    else:
        # Step 2: Validate all line items reference active items
        if not validate_line_items_active(mbo):
            # Error already raised; script stops here
            pass
        else:
            # Step 3: Calculate total cost from PO lines
            computedTotal = calculate_total_cost(mbo)
            mbo.setValue("TOTALCOST", computedTotal, FLAGS)
            logger.debug("TOTALCOST set to " + str(computedTotal))

            # Step 4: Check approval threshold
            check_approval_threshold(mbo, computedTotal)

            # Step 5: Populate currency and exchange rate from vendor
            populate_currency_from_vendor(mbo)

            # Step 6: Increment revision number if in WAPPR or APPR status
            increment_revision(mbo)

            logger.debug("PO before-save script completed successfully")

except Exception, ex:
    logger.error("Error in po.obj.save.before: " + str(ex))
    service.error("po", "saveerror")


# ==============================================================================
# scriptConfig — Launch Point & Script Registration
# ==============================================================================
scriptConfig = {
    "autoscript": "PO.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
    "description": "Calculate PO total cost, validate vendor, check approval threshold, validate line items",
    "version": "1.0",
    "active": True,
    "logLevel": "ERROR",
    "scriptLaunchPoints": [
        {
            "launchPointName": "PO_BEFORESAVE",
            "launchPointType": "OBJECT",
            "objectName": "PO",
            "save": True,
            "add": True,
            "update": True,
            "delete": False,
            "beforeSave": True,
            "active": True
        }
    ]
}
",
  "status": "Active",
  "version": "1.0",
  "loglevel": "ERROR",
  "scriptlaunchpoint": [
    {
      "launchpointname": "PO_BEFORESAVE",
      "launchpointtype": "OBJECT",
      "active": true,
      "autoscript": "PO.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE",
      "objectname": "PO",
      "add": true,
      "update": true,
      "beforesave": true
    }
  ]
}

Deploy PO.OBJ.SAVE.BEFORE

body.raw:
{
  "autoscript": "PO.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.AFTER",
  "description": "Post-PO-status cascading effects - PR linkage, inventory reserves, vendor scorecard, contract, COMMLOG",
  "scriptlanguage": "jython",
  "active": true,
  "source": """"
===============================================================================
Script Name:    po.obj.statuschange.after
Description:    Post-PO-Status Cascading Effects. Handles downstream impacts
                after a PO status change is saved, including PR linkage updates,
                inventory reservation management, vendor performance scoring,
                contract integration, and COMMLOG communication records.

Object:         PO
Launch Point:   Object Launch Point
Event:          After Save (update only)
Active:         Yes

Variables:      (None - uses MAXVARS for configuration)

Usage Notes:
    - Attach to PO object via Object Launch Point (After Save, update only)
    - PO -> APPR: Update linked PR lines, create INVRESERVE, validate contract
    - PO -> CAN: Clear PR linkage, cancel INVRESERVE
    - PO -> COMP: Vendor performance scorecard, inventory date updates
    - Every transition: Create COMMLOG entry with details
    - Vendor scorecard stored as structured data in PO longdescription

Implicit Variables:
    mbo         - Current PO MBO after save
    mboSet      - Parent MboSet
    service     - Service reference for error handling
    interactive - True if running in UI context
    user        - Current user login ID
    userInfo    - Current user's UserInfo object

Launch Point Configuration:
    1. Go to System Configuration > Automation Scripts
    2. Create Script: po.obj.statuschange.after
    3. Add Object Launch Point:
       - Object: PO
       - Event: After Save (update only)
       - Active: Yes

Change History:
    2026-03-01  Initial version - generic demo library
===============================================================================
"""

from psdi.mbo import MboConstants
from psdi.server import MXServer
from psdi.util.logging import MXLoggerFactory
from java.util import Date, Calendar
from java.text import SimpleDateFormat

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Logger setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
logger = MXLoggerFactory.getLogger("autoscript.po.obj.statuschange.after")

NOACCESSCHECK = MboConstants.NOACCESSCHECK
NOVALIDATION = MboConstants.NOVALIDATION
FLAGS = NOACCESSCHECK | NOVALIDATION


def get_status_change(poMbo):
    """
    Detect if STATUS has changed. Returns (oldStatus, newStatus) tuple.
    Returns (None, None) if STATUS did not change.
    """
    if not poMbo.getMboValueData("STATUS").isModified():
        return (None, None)

    newStatus = poMbo.getString("STATUS").upper().strip()
    oldStatus = poMbo.getMboValue("STATUS").getPreviousValue().asString()
    if oldStatus is not None:
        oldStatus = oldStatus.upper().strip()
    else:
        oldStatus = ""

    return (oldStatus, newStatus)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PR Linkage Updates
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def update_pr_linkage_on_approve(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    When PO is approved, update linked PR lines with the PONUM reference
    and update PR status to reflect PO creation.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    siteid = poMbo.getString("SITEID") if not poMbo.isNull("SITEID") else ""

    # Find POLINE records that reference a PRNUM
    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()
    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()
    prNums = set()

    while poLine is not None:
        if not poLine.isNull("PRNUM"):
            prNum = poLine.getString("PRNUM")
            prLineNum = poLine.getInt("PRLINENUM") if not poLine.isNull("PRLINENUM") else 0
            prNums.add(prNum)

            # Update the specific PRLINE with PONUM
            try:
                prLineSet = mxServer.getMboSet("PRLINE", poMbo.getUserInfo())
                whereClause = "prnum = '%s' and prlinenum = %d" % (prNum, prLineNum)
                if siteid:
                    whereClause += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
                prLineSet.setWhere(whereClause)
                prLineSet.reset()

                if not prLineSet.isEmpty():
                    prLineMbo = prLineSet.getMbo(0)
                    prLineMbo.setValue("PONUM", poNum, FLAGS)
                    prLineMbo.setValue("POLINENUM", poLine.getInt("POLINENUM"), FLAGS)
                    logger.debug(
                        "Updated PRLINE %s/%d with PONUM %s"
                        % (prNum, prLineNum, poNum)
                    )
            except Exception, ex:
                logger.warn("Failed to update PRLINE %s/%d: %s" % (prNum, prLineNum, str(ex)))

        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    # Update PR header status for each affected PR
    for prNum in prNums:
        try:
            prSet = mxServer.getMboSet("PR", poMbo.getUserInfo())
            prWhere = "prnum = '%s'" % prNum
            if siteid:
                prWhere += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
            prSet.setWhere(prWhere)
            prSet.reset()

            if not prSet.isEmpty():
                prMbo = prSet.getMbo(0)
                currentPRStatus = prMbo.getString("STATUS").upper().strip() if not prMbo.isNull("STATUS") else ""
                if currentPRStatus == "APPR":
                    prMbo.setValue("STATUS", "INPRG", FLAGS)
                    logger.info("PR %s status updated to INPRG due to PO %s approval" % (prNum, poNum))
        except Exception, ex:
            logger.warn("Failed to update PR %s status: %s" % (prNum, str(ex)))

    if len(prNums) > 0:
        logger.info(
            "Updated %d PR references for PO %s approval" % (len(prNums), poNum)
        )


def clear_pr_linkage_on_cancel(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    When PO is cancelled, clear PONUM reference on linked PRLINE records
    and revert PR line status.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    siteid = poMbo.getString("SITEID") if not poMbo.isNull("SITEID") else ""

    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()
    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()
    prNums = set()

    while poLine is not None:
        if not poLine.isNull("PRNUM"):
            prNum = poLine.getString("PRNUM")
            prLineNum = poLine.getInt("PRLINENUM") if not poLine.isNull("PRLINENUM") else 0
            prNums.add(prNum)

            try:
                prLineSet = mxServer.getMboSet("PRLINE", poMbo.getUserInfo())
                whereClause = "prnum = '%s' and prlinenum = %d" % (prNum, prLineNum)
                if siteid:
                    whereClause += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
                prLineSet.setWhere(whereClause)
                prLineSet.reset()

                if not prLineSet.isEmpty():
                    prLineMbo = prLineSet.getMbo(0)
                    prLineMbo.setValueNull("PONUM", FLAGS)
                    prLineMbo.setValueNull("POLINENUM", FLAGS)
                    logger.debug(
                        "Cleared PONUM from PRLINE %s/%d" % (prNum, prLineNum)
                    )
            except Exception, ex:
                logger.warn("Failed to clear PRLINE %s/%d: %s" % (prNum, prLineNum, str(ex)))

        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    # Revert PR status if all lines are now unlinked
    for prNum in prNums:
        try:
            prSet = mxServer.getMboSet("PR", poMbo.getUserInfo())
            prWhere = "prnum = '%s'" % prNum
            if siteid:
                prWhere += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
            prSet.setWhere(prWhere)
            prSet.reset()

            if not prSet.isEmpty():
                prMbo = prSet.getMbo(0)
                # Check if any other PRLINE still has a PONUM
                allPrLineSet = prMbo.getMboSet("PRLINE")
                allPrLineSet.setWhere("ponum is not null")
                allPrLineSet.reset()

                if allPrLineSet.isEmpty():
                    prMbo.setValue("STATUS", "APPR", FLAGS)
                    logger.info("PR %s reverted to APPR after PO %s cancellation" % (prNum, poNum))
        except Exception, ex:
            logger.warn("Failed to revert PR %s status: %s" % (prNum, str(ex)))

    if len(prNums) > 0:
        logger.info(
            "Cleared %d PR references for PO %s cancellation" % (len(prNums), poNum)
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inventory Impact
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def create_inventory_reserves_on_approve(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    When PO is approved, create INVRESERVE records for stocked items
    (POLINE.LINETYPE = 'ITEM' with STORELOC).
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    siteid = poMbo.getString("SITEID") if not poMbo.isNull("SITEID") else ""

    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()
    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()
    reserveCount = 0

    while poLine is not None:
        lineType = ""
        if not poLine.isNull("LINETYPE"):
            lineType = poLine.getString("LINETYPE").upper().strip()

        storeLoc = ""
        if not poLine.isNull("STORELOC"):
            storeLoc = poLine.getString("STORELOC").strip()

        if lineType == "ITEM" and len(storeLoc) > 0 and not poLine.isNull("ITEMNUM"):
            try:
                reserveSet = mxServer.getMboSet("INVRESERVE", poMbo.getUserInfo())
                newReserve = reserveSet.add()
                newReserve.setValue("ITEMNUM", poLine.getString("ITEMNUM"), FLAGS)
                newReserve.setValue("ITEMSETID", poLine.getString("ITEMSETID"), FLAGS)
                newReserve.setValue("STORELOC", storeLoc, FLAGS)
                newReserve.setValue("PONUM", poNum, FLAGS)
                newReserve.setValue("POLINENUM", poLine.getInt("POLINENUM"), FLAGS)
                newReserve.setValue("RESERVEDQTY", poLine.getDouble("ORDERQTY"), FLAGS)

                if siteid:
                    newReserve.setValue("SITEID", siteid, FLAGS)
                if not poMbo.isNull("ORGID"):
                    newReserve.setValue("ORGID", poMbo.getString("ORGID"), FLAGS)

                reserveCount += 1
            except Exception, ex:
                logger.warn(
                    "Failed to create INVRESERVE for PO %s line %d: %s"
                    % (poNum, poLine.getInt("POLINENUM"), str(ex))
                )

        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    if reserveCount > 0:
        logger.info(
            "Created %d INVRESERVE records for PO %s" % (reserveCount, poNum)
        )


def cancel_inventory_reserves(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    When PO is cancelled, delete INVRESERVE records tied to this PO.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    siteid = poMbo.getString("SITEID") if not poMbo.isNull("SITEID") else ""

    try:
        reserveSet = mxServer.getMboSet("INVRESERVE", poMbo.getUserInfo())
        whereClause = "ponum = '%s'" % poNum
        if siteid:
            whereClause += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
        reserveSet.setWhere(whereClause)
        reserveSet.reset()

        reserve = reserveSet.moveFirst()
        deleteCount = 0
        while reserve is not None:
            reserve.delete()
            deleteCount += 1
            reserve = reserveSet.moveNext()

        if deleteCount > 0:
            logger.info("Deleted %d INVRESERVE records for PO %s" % (deleteCount, poNum))
    except Exception, ex:
        logger.warn("Failed to cancel INVRESERVE for PO %s: %s" % (poNum, str(ex)))


def update_inventory_on_complete(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    When PO is completed, update INVENTORY.LASTISSUEDATE and
    expected receipt data for stocked items.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    siteid = poMbo.getString("SITEID") if not poMbo.isNull("SITEID") else ""

    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()
    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()
    updateCount = 0

    while poLine is not None:
        lineType = ""
        if not poLine.isNull("LINETYPE"):
            lineType = poLine.getString("LINETYPE").upper().strip()

        if lineType == "ITEM" and not poLine.isNull("ITEMNUM") and not poLine.isNull("STORELOC"):
            itemNum = poLine.getString("ITEMNUM")
            storeLoc = poLine.getString("STORELOC")
            itemSetId = poLine.getString("ITEMSETID") if not poLine.isNull("ITEMSETID") else ""

            try:
                invSet = mxServer.getMboSet("INVENTORY", poMbo.getUserInfo())
                invWhere = "itemnum = '%s' and location = '%s'" % (itemNum, storeLoc)
                if siteid:
                    invWhere += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
                if itemSetId:
                    invWhere += " and itemsetid = '%s'" % itemSetId
                invSet.setWhere(invWhere)
                invSet.reset()

                if not invSet.isEmpty():
                    invMbo = invSet.getMbo(0)
                    invMbo.setValue("LASTISSUEDATE", Date(), FLAGS)
                    updateCount += 1
            except Exception, ex:
                logger.warn(
                    "Failed to update INVENTORY for item %s at %s: %s"
                    % (itemNum, storeLoc, str(ex))
                )

        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    if updateCount > 0:
        logger.info(
            "Updated %d INVENTORY records for PO %s completion" % (updateCount, poNum)
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Vendor Performance Tracking
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def calculate_vendor_scorecard(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    On PO completion, calculate vendor performance metrics:
    - ON_TIME_DELIVERY: RECEIPTDATE vs PROMISEDATE comparison
    - QUALITY_SCORE: Check for rejected receipts (REJECTQTY > 0)
    - PRICE_ACCURACY: Compare invoiced unit cost vs original unit cost
    - FILL_RATE: RECEIVEDQTY / ORDERQTY across all lines
    Store scorecard as structured data in PO longdescription.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    siteid = poMbo.getString("SITEID") if not poMbo.isNull("SITEID") else ""
    vendor = poMbo.getString("VENDOR") if not poMbo.isNull("VENDOR") else "UNKNOWN"

    totalLines = 0
    onTimeLines = 0
    lateLines = 0
    totalOrderQty = 0.0
    totalReceivedQty = 0.0
    totalRejectQty = 0.0
    priceVarianceSum = 0.0
    priceVarianceCount = 0

    # Iterate POLINE for fill rate and promise date comparison
    poLineSet = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
    poLineSet.reset()
    poLine = poLineSet.moveFirst()

    while poLine is not None:
        totalLines += 1
        orderQty = poLine.getDouble("ORDERQTY") if not poLine.isNull("ORDERQTY") else 0.0
        receivedQty = poLine.getDouble("RECEIVEDQTY") if not poLine.isNull("RECEIVEDQTY") else 0.0
        totalOrderQty += orderQty
        totalReceivedQty += receivedQty

        # Check promise date vs actual receipt
        lineNum = poLine.getInt("POLINENUM")
        if not poLine.isNull("PROMISEDATE"):
            promiseDate = poLine.getDate("PROMISEDATE")

            # Look up receipt date from MATRECTRANS
            try:
                receiptSet = mxServer.getMboSet("MATRECTRANS", poMbo.getUserInfo())
                rWhere = "ponum = '%s' and polinenum = %d" % (poMbo.getString("PONUM"), lineNum)
                if siteid:
                    rWhere += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
                receiptSet.setWhere(rWhere)
                receiptSet.setOrderBy("ACTUALDATE DESC")
                receiptSet.reset()

                if not receiptSet.isEmpty():
                    receiptMbo = receiptSet.getMbo(0)
                    if not receiptMbo.isNull("ACTUALDATE"):
                        actualDate = receiptMbo.getDate("ACTUALDATE")
                        if actualDate.after(promiseDate):
                            lateLines += 1
                        else:
                            onTimeLines += 1
            except Exception, ex:
                logger.debug("Receipt lookup failed for line %d: %s" % (lineNum, str(ex)))

        poLine = poLineSet.moveNext()

    # Check rejected receipts for quality score
    try:
        rejectSet = mxServer.getMboSet("MATRECTRANS", poMbo.getUserInfo())
        rejWhere = "ponum = '%s' and rejectqty > 0" % poMbo.getString("PONUM")
        if siteid:
            rejWhere += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
        rejectSet.setWhere(rejWhere)
        rejectSet.reset()

        rejectMbo = rejectSet.moveFirst()
        while rejectMbo is not None:
            totalRejectQty += rejectMbo.getDouble("REJECTQTY") if not rejectMbo.isNull("REJECTQTY") else 0.0
            rejectMbo = rejectSet.moveNext()
    except Exception, ex:
        logger.debug("Reject qty lookup failed: " + str(ex))

    # Price accuracy from invoice lines
    try:
        poLineSet2 = poMbo.getMboSet("POLINE")
        poLineSet2.reset()
        pl = poLineSet2.moveFirst()
        while pl is not None:
            origCost = pl.getDouble("UNITCOST") if not pl.isNull("UNITCOST") else 0.0
            lineNum2 = pl.getInt("POLINENUM")

            if origCost > 0:
                invLineSet = mxServer.getMboSet("INVOICELINE", poMbo.getUserInfo())
                invWhere = "ponum = '%s' and polinenum = %d" % (poMbo.getString("PONUM"), lineNum2)
                if siteid:
                    invWhere += " and siteid = '%s'" % siteid
                invLineSet.setWhere(invWhere)
                invLineSet.reset()

                if not invLineSet.isEmpty():
                    invLineMbo = invLineSet.getMbo(0)
                    invoicedCost = invLineMbo.getDouble("UNITCOST") if not invLineMbo.isNull("UNITCOST") else 0.0
                    if invoicedCost > 0:
                        variance = abs(invoicedCost - origCost) / origCost * 100.0
                        priceVarianceSum += variance
                        priceVarianceCount += 1

            pl = poLineSet2.moveNext()
    except Exception, ex:
        logger.debug("Price accuracy calculation failed: " + str(ex))

    # Calculate scores
    deliveryLinesChecked = onTimeLines + lateLines
    onTimeDeliveryPct = (float(onTimeLines) / float(deliveryLinesChecked) * 100.0) if deliveryLinesChecked > 0 else 100.0
    qualityScore = 100.0 - ((totalRejectQty / totalReceivedQty * 100.0) if totalReceivedQty > 0 else 0.0)
    qualityScore = max(0.0, qualityScore)
    avgPriceVariance = (priceVarianceSum / float(priceVarianceCount)) if priceVarianceCount > 0 else 0.0
    priceAccuracy = max(0.0, 100.0 - avgPriceVariance)
    fillRate = (totalReceivedQty / totalOrderQty * 100.0) if totalOrderQty > 0 else 0.0

    # Build scorecard text
    sdf = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
    timestamp = sdf.format(Date())

    scorecard = (
        "\n--- VENDOR PERFORMANCE SCORECARD ---\n"
        "Date: %s\n"
        "Vendor: %s\n"
        "PO: %s\n"
        "Total Lines: %d\n"
        "ON_TIME_DELIVERY: %.1f%% (%d on-time, %d late of %d checked)\n"
        "QUALITY_SCORE: %.1f%% (reject qty: %.1f of %.1f received)\n"
        "PRICE_ACCURACY: %.1f%% (avg variance: %.2f%%)\n"
        "FILL_RATE: %.1f%% (received: %.1f of %.1f ordered)\n"
        "---\n"
        % (timestamp, vendor, poNum, totalLines,
           onTimeDeliveryPct, onTimeLines, lateLines, deliveryLinesChecked,
           qualityScore, totalRejectQty, totalReceivedQty,
           priceAccuracy, avgPriceVariance,
           fillRate, totalReceivedQty, totalOrderQty)
    )

    # Append to longdescription
    existingDesc = ""
    if not poMbo.isNull("DESCRIPTION_LONGDESCRIPTION"):
        existingDesc = poMbo.getString("DESCRIPTION_LONGDESCRIPTION")

    poMbo.setValue("DESCRIPTION_LONGDESCRIPTION", existingDesc + scorecard, FLAGS)

    logger.info(
        "Vendor scorecard for PO %s: delivery=%.1f%%, quality=%.1f%%, price=%.1f%%, fill=%.1f%%"
        % (poNum, onTimeDeliveryPct, qualityScore, priceAccuracy, fillRate)
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Contract Integration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def validate_and_update_contract(poMbo, poNum):
    """
    On PO approval, if CONTRACTREFNUM is set:
    - Validate the referenced contract is active
    - Update contract committed amount
    - Check if PO would exceed contract ceiling
    """
    if poMbo.isNull("CONTRACTREFNUM"):
        return

    contractRef = poMbo.getString("CONTRACTREFNUM")
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    orgId = poMbo.getString("ORGID") if not poMbo.isNull("ORGID") else ""

    try:
        contractSet = mxServer.getMboSet("CONTRACT", poMbo.getUserInfo())
        cWhere = "contractnum = '%s'" % contractRef
        if orgId:
            cWhere += " and orgid = '%s'" % orgId
        contractSet.setWhere(cWhere)
        contractSet.reset()

        if contractSet.isEmpty():
            logger.warn(
                "Contract %s referenced by PO %s not found" % (contractRef, poNum)
            )
            return

        contractMbo = contractSet.getMbo(0)

        # Check contract is active
        contractStatus = ""
        if not contractMbo.isNull("STATUS"):
            contractStatus = contractMbo.getString("STATUS").upper().strip()

        if contractStatus != "APPR":
            logger.warn(
                "Contract %s is not active (status: %s) for PO %s"
                % (contractRef, contractStatus, poNum)
            )
            return

        # Get PO total cost
        poTotal = poMbo.getDouble("TOTALCOST") if not poMbo.isNull("TOTALCOST") else 0.0

        # Check contract ceiling
        contractMax = 0.0
        if not contractMbo.isNull("TOTALCOST"):
            contractMax = contractMbo.getDouble("TOTALCOST")

        currentCommitted = 0.0
        if not contractMbo.isNull("COMMITTEDCOST"):
            currentCommitted = contractMbo.getDouble("COMMITTEDCOST")

        if contractMax > 0 and (currentCommitted + poTotal) > contractMax:
            logger.warn(
                "PO %s total %s would exceed contract %s ceiling %s (current committed: %s)"
                % (poNum, str(poTotal), contractRef, str(contractMax), str(currentCommitted))
            )
            # Log warning but do not block -- contract ceiling is advisory
        else:
            # Update contract committed amount
            contractMbo.setValue("COMMITTEDCOST", currentCommitted + poTotal, FLAGS)
            logger.info(
                "Contract %s committed cost updated: %s -> %s for PO %s"
                % (contractRef, str(currentCommitted), str(currentCommitted + poTotal), poNum)
            )

    except Exception, ex:
        logger.warn("Contract integration failed for PO %s: %s" % (poNum, str(ex)))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# COMMLOG Entry
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def create_commlog_entry(poMbo, poNum, oldStatus, newStatus):
    """
    Create a COMMLOG entry for the PO status transition with full details.
    """
    mxServer = MXServer.getMXServer()
    sdf = SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
    timestamp = sdf.format(Date())
    currentUser = user if user is not None else "SYSTEM"

    totalCost = poMbo.getDouble("TOTALCOST") if not poMbo.isNull("TOTALCOST") else 0.0
    vendor = poMbo.getString("VENDOR") if not poMbo.isNull("VENDOR") else ""

    subject = "PO %s Status Change: %s -> %s" % (poNum, oldStatus, newStatus)
    message = (
        "Purchase Order: %s\n"
        "Status Change: %s -> %s\n"
        "Date/Time: %s\n"
        "Changed By: %s\n"
        "Vendor: %s\n"
        "Total Cost: %s\n"
        % (poNum, oldStatus, newStatus, timestamp, currentUser, vendor, str(totalCost))
    )

    try:
        commlogSet = mxServer.getMboSet("COMMLOG", poMbo.getUserInfo())
        commlog = commlogSet.add()
        commlog.setValue("OWNERTABLE", "PO", FLAGS)
        commlog.setValue("OWNERID", poMbo.getLong("POID"), FLAGS)
        commlog.setValue("SENDTO", currentUser, FLAGS)
        commlog.setValue("SUBJECT", subject, FLAGS)
        commlog.setValue("MESSAGE", message, FLAGS)
        commlog.setValue("SENDFROM", "SYSTEM", FLAGS)
        commlog.setValue("LOGTYPE", "STATUS", FLAGS)

        logger.info("COMMLOG created for PO %s: %s -> %s" % (poNum, oldStatus, newStatus))

    except Exception, ex:
        logger.warn("Failed to create COMMLOG for PO %s: %s" % (poNum, str(ex)))


# ===========================================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION
# ===========================================================================

try:
    poNum = mbo.getString("PONUM") if not mbo.isNull("PONUM") else "UNKNOWN"
    logger.debug("PO status change after-save starting for PO: " + poNum)

    oldStatus, newStatus = get_status_change(mbo)

    if oldStatus is None or newStatus is None:
        logger.debug("No status change detected for PO %s; skipping" % poNum)
    else:
        logger.info(
            "PO %s post-status-change processing: %s -> %s" % (poNum, oldStatus, newStatus)
        )

        # --- PO -> APPR: PR linkage, inventory reserves, contract ---
        if newStatus == "APPR":
            update_pr_linkage_on_approve(mbo, poNum)
            create_inventory_reserves_on_approve(mbo, poNum)
            validate_and_update_contract(mbo, poNum)

        # --- PO -> CAN: Clear PR linkage, cancel reserves ---
        elif newStatus == "CAN":
            clear_pr_linkage_on_cancel(mbo, poNum)
            cancel_inventory_reserves(mbo, poNum)

        # --- PO -> COMP: Vendor scorecard, inventory updates ---
        elif newStatus == "COMP":
            calculate_vendor_scorecard(mbo, poNum)
            update_inventory_on_complete(mbo, poNum)

        # --- PO -> INPRG: Lightweight logging only ---
        elif newStatus == "INPRG":
            logger.info("PO %s moved to In Progress" % poNum)

        # --- PO -> CLOSE: Final logging ---
        elif newStatus == "CLOSE":
            logger.info("PO %s closed" % poNum)

        # --- PO -> REVISE: Logging ---
        elif newStatus == "REVISE":
            revNum = mbo.getInt("REVISIONNUM") if not mbo.isNull("REVISIONNUM") else 0
            logger.info("PO %s moved to Revision %d" % (poNum, revNum))

        # --- COMMLOG for every transition ---
        create_commlog_entry(mbo, poNum, oldStatus, newStatus)

        logger.info(
            "PO %s post-status-change processing completed: %s -> %s"
            % (poNum, oldStatus, newStatus)
        )

except Exception, ex:
    # After-save errors should not block the transaction -- log and continue
    logger.error("Error in po.obj.statuschange.after: " + str(ex))


# ==============================================================================
# scriptConfig -- Launch Point & Script Registration
# ==============================================================================
scriptConfig = {
    "autoscript": "PO.OBJ.STATUSCHANGE.AFTER",
    "description": "Post-PO-status cascading effects - PR linkage, inventory reserves, vendor scorecard, contract, COMMLOG",
    "version": "1.0",
    "active": True,
    "logLevel": "ERROR",
    "scriptLaunchPoints": [
        {
            "launchPointName": "PO_STATUSCHANGE_AFTERSAVE",
            "launchPointType": "OBJECT",
            "objectName": "PO",
            "save": True,
            "add": False,
            "update": True,
            "delete": False,
            "afterSave": True,
            "active": True
        }
    ]
}

  "loglevel": "ERROR",

      "launchpointtype": "OBJECT",
      "active": true,
